parameterGroups.label: Transpose
parameterGroups.rationale: Single parameter controlling the semitone offset applied to MIDI notes

parameters.name: Transpose Amount
parameters.description: Semitone offset applied to incoming note-on events

gaps.question: Does processHiseEvent() transpose only note-on events, or does it also transpose the corresponding note-off events to ensure matched pairs? Does it affect any other MIDI event types?
gaps.context: The base description says 'note-on events' only. If note-off events are not also transposed, notes would never release properly. Need to verify and correct the description if necessary.

gaps.question: If TransposeAmount is changed while notes are held, do the currently sounding notes shift, or does the new value only apply to subsequent note-on events?
gaps.context: Users need to know whether this is safe to automate during playback or only intended as a static offset.

gaps.question: What happens when transposition pushes a note number outside the valid MIDI range (0-127)? Is the note clamped, wrapped, or discarded?
gaps.context: Edge case that affects usability at extreme transpose values near the top or bottom of the keyboard.

gaps.question: The base description says 'Transposes incoming MIDI note-on events'. If note-off events are also transposed (as expected), the description should say 'MIDI note events' or 'note-on and note-off events'.
gaps.context: Inaccurate description could mislead users into thinking note-off handling is broken or absent.

notes: Transposer is one of the simplest MIDI processors - a single parameter with integer steps. The description mentioning only 'note-on events' is almost certainly incomplete since untransposed note-off events would leave notes stuck. This should be verified and corrected in Step 3. No interfaces, no modulation chains, no FX chain, no child processors.
